$comment: #297: Reference-task catalog for the eval harness. Each entry is one task that gets run against each preset (or a subset). Add new tasks by appending to the array; the harness picks them up on next run. Keep tasks small so the full sweep finishes in a few hours.

tasks.title: Audit README claims for accuracy
tasks.directive: Read the README. For each feature claimed, verify the code actually implements it. List discrepancies (claims with no implementation; implementation with no docs).
tasks.$rationale: Pure analysis task. Tests whether each preset can produce a coherent audit list. Blackboard included because its planner can extract auditable claims into todos.

tasks.title: Resolve TODO/FIXME comments
tasks.directive: Find every TODO, FIXME, and XXX comment in src/. For each, either resolve it (commit a fix) or convert it to an issue-tracker reference. Be conservative — only fix when the resolution is clear from context.
tasks.$rationale: Code-modification task. Blackboard-only because other presets are discussion-only. Tight scope (per-comment small commits) suits the worker pipeline.

tasks.title: Choose: stay with Express or migrate to Fastify?
tasks.directive: Evaluate whether this project should keep using Express or migrate to Fastify. Consider performance, ecosystem maturity, migration cost, and team familiarity. Produce a clear recommendation with rationale.
tasks.proposition: This project should migrate from Express to Fastify.
tasks.$rationale: Decision task with two clear sides. Council parallel-drafts pros/cons; debate-judge formal PRO/CON; role-diff different angles. Tests preset suitability for architectural decisions.

tasks.title: Build a coverage map of which modules are well-tested vs not
tasks.directive: Walk the repo. For each source file, identify whether it has corresponding tests, what fraction of its API surface is covered, and where the test gaps are. Produce a coverage table.
tasks.$rationale: Repo-walk task. Stigmergy's pheromone-trail design fits naturally; map-reduce's per-file slicing also works; round-robin baseline.

tasks.title: Fix the countDown off-by-one (fixture mode)
tasks.directive: src/countdown.js has a loop that exits one iteration too early — countDown(3) returns [3, 2] instead of [3, 2, 1]. Change the loop terminator so the returned array includes the final 1. Make the smallest possible edit; do not refactor.
tasks.$rationale: First fixture-mode catalog entry — exercises end-to-end fixture staging + verify.

tasks.title: Add a null-guard so formatUser(null) doesn't throw
tasks.directive: src/format.js exports formatUser(input). It currently dereferences input.name and input.email without checking input — calling formatUser(null) throws. Make it safe: when input is null or undefined, return the string 'Anonymous <unknown>'. Preserve the happy path exactly.
tasks.$rationale: Defensive-coding fixture. Tests whether the swarm/baseline can add an early-return without breaking the happy path. Verify exercises null + undefined fallback.

tasks.title: Extract inlined tax math into a pure helper applyTax(amount)
tasks.directive: src/calc.js has computePrice(items) with the tax math inlined (subtotal + subtotal * 0.07). Extract that into a pure helper named applyTax(amount) returning amount * 1.07. computePrice should call applyTax. Behavior must be preserved exactly; the exported computePrice signature must not change.
tasks.$rationale: Refactor fixture. Tests whether the swarm/baseline can extract a pure helper while preserving behavior. Verify checks computePrice still works AND applyTax exists.

tasks.title: Rename oldSum to sum across src/
tasks.directive: Multiple files under src/ import + use a function named oldSum. Rename it to sum everywhere — both the export site (src/sum.js) and every import site. After the rename, NO file under src/ should reference the old name oldSum, and the renamed function must still be callable and produce the correct result (1+2=3).
tasks.$rationale: Multi-file consistency edit. Tests whether the agent finds + updates ALL call sites, not just the export.

tasks.title: Fix the wrong assertion in src/sum.test.mjs
tasks.directive: src/sum.test.mjs has a broken assertion: sum(2,3) is asserted to equal 6. The production function src/sum.js is correct (returns 5). Fix the assertion (or test description) so all tests pass — WITHOUT modifying src/sum.js. The test must reflect the function's actual behavior.
tasks.$rationale: Constraint-respecting fix. Tests whether the agent honors the 'do not modify src/sum.js' constraint and changes only the test assertion.

tasks.title: Add a Usage section to tinypkg's README.md
tasks.directive: The tinypkg README.md is missing a `## Usage` section that documents how to call the exported function. Read the package source to understand the function's signature, then add a Usage section to README.md with at least one minimal code example.
tasks.$rationale: Doc-writing task with code-grounding. Tests whether the agent reads source to write accurate docs (vs hallucinating an API).

tasks.title: Scan src/ + write a JSON report of console.log calls
tasks.directive: Walk src/ and find every console.log call. Write report.json at the repo root with this shape: {"count": N, "calls": [{"file": "src/main.js", "line": 7}, ...]}. Lists must contain real file paths + 1-based line numbers; count must equal calls.length.
tasks.$rationale: Analysis-style task: scan + structured-emit. Tests whether the agent can produce JSON with file:line precision (not hallucinated lines).

tasks.title: Read package.json + emit a categorized dep report
tasks.directive: Read package.json. Write categories.json at the repo root with three lists — `runtime`, `dev`, `optional` — each containing the dependency NAMES (not version strings) from `dependencies`, `devDependencies`, and `optionalDependencies` respectively.
tasks.$rationale: Structured-data extraction. Tests whether the agent reads JSON correctly + emits the right shape (names not versions, three buckets).

tasks.title: Create entry point + wire npm script
tasks.directive: Two steps: (1) Create src/main.js that imports `greet` from ./greeter.js and calls greet("world"), printing the result. (2) Add a `greet` script to package.json that invokes src/main.js (canonical: `"greet": "node src/main.js"`). When `npm run greet` runs, it should print `hello, world!`.
tasks.$rationale: Multi-file change requiring two coordinated edits. Single-shot baseline often hits one half but not the other — good test of swarm sequencing.

tasks.title: Add a verbose option to formatLog + write the test
tasks.directive: Two steps: (1) In src/log.js, extend formatLog(level, msg, opts) so that when opts.verbose === true the returned string includes a [v] marker after the level (e.g. "INFO [v]: hello"). Default behavior unchanged when opts.verbose is absent or false. (2) Create src/log.test.mjs using node:test that asserts BOTH the verbose-true case AND the default-no-marker case. Tests must pass.
tasks.$rationale: Code-and-test paired change. Tests both implementation skill AND test-writing skill in one task.

$presetOverrides.$comment: Per-preset minimum-agent overrides. Catalog tasks can specify smaller agentCount; the harness clamps up to preset.min.
